_meta.source: Teach Your Child to Read in 100 Easy Lessons (sound symbols & phoneme segmentation only; no lesson scripts reproduced)
_meta.extractedRange: Lessons 1-50 (decodable word lists + stories). Phoneme inventory covers all sounds through Lesson 89. Per-word 'units'; ck=single /k/; vowel digraphs (ea/ee/oa/ai/ear/or) = single tap reusing long-vowel clips (Option A).
_meta.notation: Each word is an array of taught sound-symbols. Long vowels carry a macron (e,a,o,i,u,y). 'th','sh','ch','ng','ar','er','oo','ea','ai','ou','wh','qu' are single joined sounds. Doubled letters in print that map to one sound (e.g. 'tt' in mitt) are one segment.
_meta.segmentationNote: Segmentations marked verified were read directly from the book's dotted-arrow markings. The app blends the final tap into the whole word.

story.text: see me eat

decodableWords.note: book prints joined e+ar before ar is formally introduced

story.text: see me read
mad at me
read it
that rat is sad
sam is mad at me
see the ram sit
the ram is sad
this cat is sad
that is a seed. see a ram eat it.
this is a rock. sam is at the rock.
a rat is in a sack. that rat is not sad.
the sock is near a man. a cat is in that sock.
a man sat on a ram. that ram can not see.
an ant is fat. it can sit and eat.
a sock is in the sun. the sock is on me.
an ant can eat a seed. that seed is in the mud.

decodableWords.note: final 'le' = /l/; 'tt' one sound; dot count unclear on scan

story.text: this is a cat. the cat can run. mud is on the cat.

decodableWords.note: final 'le' = /l/; 'tt' one sound; dot count unclear on scan

story.text: a man sat in the sand. a little ant can see the man. the ant is mad.
this little cat can run in sand. that little cat can sit on sand. see the feet.
we see a duck. we can sit in the sun with that duck. it is fun in the sun.
we will run. I will win. I am not a duck. I am an ant.

decodableWords.note: final 'le' = /l/; 'tt' one sound; dot count unclear on scan

story.text: a cat sat on a little rug. the cat got mud on that rug. mom got mad at the cat.

decodableWords.note: irregular, says /sed/; 'ai' printed before formal intro at L89

story.text: I am a log. I can not run. I can not sit on an ant. an ant will sit on me.
see that little shack. sand is in the shack. we will run in the sand.

decodableWords.note: ai = joined long-a; printed before formal intro at L89

story.text: a little cat can sit on a rug. she can run in the sand. she can lick a man. she will lick me.

decodableWords.note: ow = /ou/; printed before formal intro at L89
ow = /ou/; printed before formal intro at L89
ow = /ou/; printed before formal intro at L89

story.text: a little fish sat on a fat fish. the little fish said, "wow." the little fish did not feel sad. the little fish said, "that fat fish is mom."
the cow sat on a little gate. the cow said, "the gate is hot." she said, "I hate hot gates." she said, "I will run now."
a fish ate a rock. the fish said, "I ate a rock." a cow ate the fish. the cow said, "I ate a fish. and now I feel sick."
she can kick. she can lick. she said, "I am not a cat." she said, "I am not a fish." is she a man?

he said, "can I eat cake?" she said, "go sit with the cow." he said, "no. I will not go." she said, "go sit with the cat." he said, "the cat has cake." she said, "go sit with the cat and eat cake." so he ate cake. he said, "this is fun."

decodableWords.note: '-en' second syllable; segmentation uncertain on scan

story.text: he has no feet. he has no nose. he has no teeth. he is not a cow. and he is not a cat. is he a rat? no. he is not a rat.
I can kiss a cat. I can kiss a kitten. can a cow kiss me? no. a cow can not kiss me. a cow can lick me. can a cat lick a kitten?
we save rocks. we save sacks and sacks of rocks. we save lots and lots of rocks. we have lots of little rocks. can we sit on rocks? and we give an old man lots of rocks.
he said, "give me a rock or a sock." so she gave him a sock on his nose. he said, "I need socks on the feet, not on the nose." so she gave him socks for his feet.
the old man was cold. he did not have a hat or a coat or socks. so he got a goat with lots of hats and coats and socks. now the old man is not cold. and the goat is not cold.
a man gave an old coat to an old goat. that old goat said, "I will eat this old coat." so he did. "that was fun," he said. "I ate an old coat. and now I am cold." now the old goat is sad.

she said, "I have a fan." he said, "I have lots of sand." she said, "we can run the sand in that fan." so he ran the fan near the sand. he had sand in his ears. he said, "I can not hear." he had sand on his seat. she said, "we have sand on us."
